pi_items.source: Task description requires creating a workflow action named 'AbuseIPDB_Check', enforced by verifier.py logic matching the normalized name.
Task description specifies the URL 'https://www.abuseipdb.com/check/$src_ip$', enforced by verifier.py checking if 'abuseipdb.com' is in the URI.
Task description requires creating a workflow action named 'Deep_IP_Investigation', enforced by verifier.py logic matching the normalized name.
Task description requires creating a saved search named 'Top_Attacking_IPs', enforced by verifier.py logic matching the normalized name.
Task description requires querying the 'security_logs' index, enforced by verifier.py checking if 'security_logs' is in the search string.
Task description specifies credentials admin / SplunkAdmin1!. install_splunk.sh configures Splunk with USERNAME = admin.
Task description specifies credentials admin / SplunkAdmin1!. install_splunk.sh configures Splunk with PASSWORD = SplunkAdmin1!.

privileged_info_summary: The environment uses real-world datasets downloaded during setup from SecRepo (auth.log) and Loghub (Linux syslog, SSH, Apache). The task explicitly mandates the creation of three specific objects using exact names and configurations: a workflow action named 'AbuseIPDB_Check' pointing to abuseipdb.com, a workflow action named 'Deep_IP_Investigation', and a saved search named 'Top_Attacking_IPs' targeting the 'security_logs' index. The agent must use the Splunk web interface at localhost:8000 using the pre-configured credentials (admin/SplunkAdmin1!) to successfully complete the task.
